perturbation.original_text: You agree to transmit the requested information that is on your books and records to the Fund or its designee promptly, but in any event not later than five (5) business days, after receipt of a request.
perturbation.changed_text: You agree to transmit the requested information that is on your books and records to the Fund or its designee no later than ten (10) business days, after receipt of a request.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation modifies the deadline for transmitting shareholder information in Section 18.1.2 from five (5) business days to ten (10) business days. This directly contradicts Section 18.2.2, which states instructions be executed not later than five business days creating an in-text contradiction regarding the timeline for responding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You agree to execute instructions as soon as reasonably practicable, but not later than five business days after your receipt of the instructions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the deadline for transmitting information from five (5) business days to ten (10) business days. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states instructions must be executed not later than five business days. This is a direct conflict regarding the timeline, causing an inconsistency as described by the Perturbation Explanation, and falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated as to the payments made on behalf of the Funds at any time, without the payment of any penalty, by the vote of a majority of the members of the Board of Trustees and who have no direct or indirect financial interest in the operation of the Administrative Services Plan or in any related agreements to the Administrative Services Plan ("Disinterested Trustees") or by a majority of the outstanding voting securities of a Fund, on at least sixty (60) days written notice to the parties to this Agreement. In addition, either you or Nationwide may terminate this Agreement (i) upon the material breach of this Agreement by the other or (ii) for any reason on at least ninety (90) days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated as to the payments made on behalf of the Funds at any time, without the payment of any penalty, by the vote of a majority of the members of the Board of Trustees and who have no direct or indirect financial interest in the operation of the Administrative Services Plan or in any related agreements to the Administrative Services Plan ("Disinterested Trustees") or by a majority of the outstanding voting securities of a Fund, on at least thirty (30) days written notice to the parties to this Agreement. In addition, either you or Nationwide may terminate this Agreement (i) upon the material breach of this Agreement by the other or (ii) for any reason on at least one hundred and twenty (120) days written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction in Section 9 by altering the notice periods for termination. The Board of Trustees now has 30 days for termination notice (down from 60), but Nationwide/Servicing Agent has 120 days notice for termination (up from 90). This creates in-text contradiction as to which notice period applies in different scenarios, particularly impacting termination rights and obligations of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be terminated as to the payments made on behalf of the Funds at any time, without the payment of any penalty, by the vote of a majority of the members of the Board of Trustees and who have no direct or indirect financial interest in the operation of the Administrative Services Plan or in any related agreements to the Administrative Services Plan ("Disinterested Trustees") or by a majority of the outstanding voting securities of a Fund, on at least thirty (30) days written notice to the parties to this Agreement. In addition, either you or Nationwide may terminate this Agreement (i) upon the material breach of this Agreement by the other or (ii) for any reason on at least one hundred and twenty (120) days written notice to the other party. 3 In the event this Agreement is terminated as described herein, the indemnification provisions contained in this Agreement shall continue until the possibilities for damages or loss have expired.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces conflicting notice periods for termination. The Board of Trustees now has 30 days' notice, while Nationwide/Servicing Agent has 120 days. The contradicted text reflects this change, but the change itself creates a contradiction within the same section of the agreement regarding which notice period applies, making the agreement inconsistent and triggering an 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as defined.
